question:
What is the central tension in the social norm that people should "earn" respect rather than receive it by default?
A) Respect cannot be earned through any observable behavior.
B) It legally conflicts with constitutional protections in most countries.
C) While earned respect may feel more meaningful, treating people with baseline dignity regardless of status is also a broadly held value — meaning the two norms frequently conflict.
D) The norm has been universally abandoned in modern societies.
